reason: Response B has the structural scaffolding, dense and accurate concrete examples (Greek temple entasis, fukinsei, rule of thirds), and the both/and resolution reframed around a precise superset claim ("balance, of which symmetry is only the simplest special case") that match how I actually handle essay prompts, culminating in a crisp aphoristic synthesis ("symmetry is more immediately pleasing, asymmetry more lastingly pleasing"). Response A reads as a performatively literary imitation — heavy on rhetorical flourishes ("And yet. And yet."), emotional crescendo, and a one-sided verdict — which is closer to a pastiche of a "deep essay" voice than my typical analytic, example-marshaling register.
